_note: SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURE — not a real published brief. URLs are example.org placeholders and the source text is illustrative. In production, sources come from live retrieval (WebSearch / web_fetch / research MCPs) and claims from an LLM compose step; this engine VERIFIES them. See README.

sources.title: Illustrative hydration reference (example)
sources.text: Drinking water supports normal body temperature regulation during exercise. Thirst is a reasonable early guide to fluid needs for most people in typical conditions.

sources.title: Illustrative electrolyte reference (example)
sources.text: Electrolyte loss through sweat can affect performance in long endurance events lasting more than about an hour.

claims.text: Water helps regulate body temperature during exercise.

citations.quote: supports normal body temperature regulation during exercise

claims.text: For most people in typical conditions, thirst is a reasonable early guide to fluid needs.

citations.quote: Thirst is a reasonable early guide to fluid needs

claims.text: In endurance events lasting over about an hour, electrolyte loss from sweat can affect performance.

citations.quote: Electrolyte loss through sweat can affect performance in long endurance events

uncertainty: Individual fluid and electrolyte needs vary with body size, climate, and intensity; these are general patterns, not a personal prescription.
The sources here are illustrative examples; a production brief would cite live, retrievable references.
